summary: Added end-to-end source persistence for paper trades with Trade.source defaulting to 'bot', DB migration for trades.source, paper_exec passthrough, dashboard API sourcing from DB, and state.json source surfacing.
Created core/model_bridge.py with default-disabled kill switch and guarded latest-per-slug bridge gating that discards NO_TRADE before evaluation and emits BRIDGE GATE PASS/REJECT logs.
Added a minimal additive bridge hook to bot_core.py that can open paper trades via existing paper_exec using source='model_bridge' when the bridge is manually enabled later.
schema_changes: Added Trade.source with default 'bot' in core/types.py.
Added additive SQLite migration in core/db.py: ALTER TABLE trades ADD COLUMN source TEXT DEFAULT 'bot' guarded by OperationalError.
Updated insert_open_trade/fetch_open_trades/fetch_recent_closed/_row_to_trade to persist and read the source column.
gates_implemented: ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE=False kill switch returns [] and logs BRIDGE DISABLED
latest actionable recommendation per market_slug only
action must be BUY_YES or BUY_NO
one intent per slug per loop
tests_run: Ran bot_core.py and observed DB migration log line: 'DB migration: added source column'.
Confirmed kill switch behavior path is present in core/model_bridge.py and left ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE=False in the submitted patch.
Ran dashboard_server.py and confirmed server started on http://localhost:8900 without regression.
Checked runtime/state.json after patching to confirm existing positions file remained readable; it did not yet refresh during the short verification window.
result: Files patched successfully and bot/dashboard both started after the change set.
Kill switch default remains OFF in submitted patch.
Dedup logic is implemented in model_bridge.py before gating by collapsing to the latest entry per slug and skipping NO_TRADE records.
source='model_bridge' is wired through Trade -> DB -> dashboard API/state path for future bridge-created paper trades; existing trades default to source='bot'.
risks: I did not manually flip ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE=True during this run, so no live BRIDGE GATE PASS/REJECT lines were observed in process output and no new model_bridge position was created during verification.
runtime/state.json shown during verification was still the prior snapshot because bot_core.py was terminated before a fresh loop/state write completed.

bot_core bridge execution uses market lookup from current discovery set by slug; if a gated intent references a slug absent from current discovery, it logs a market_lookup rejection instead of opening a trade.
next_recommended_task: Run PAPER_BRIDGE_003 verification pass with ENABLE_MODEL_BRIDGE temporarily set True and capture BRIDGE GATE PASS/REJECT plus a source=model_bridge position in state/api.
